diffCoverage.notes: Verified the new session layer sits between zalo and command in the forward-only order and that command no longer holds session state directly.

resolvedFindings.resolution: The command-layer ListenerState { HashMap<AccountId, Arc<API>> + Vec<Listener> } is removed; session state now lives in the dedicated session/ layer as SessionManager, resolving the architecture follow-up logged on send-text and listener-events.

findings.evidence: session/ depends only on types and zalo (lower layers) and is consumed by command (higher). The realtime listener is abstracted behind the MessageListener trait, implemented for zca-rust's Listener inside this layer via crate::zalo::Listener, so zca-rust concrete types stay confined to zalo and are not re-exposed above session. command/mod.rs imports crate::session::SessionManager and crate::zalo only for the API/send facade. Forward-only DAG (types → config → store → zalo → session → command) preserved.
findings.fix: None required. When persistence-driven lifecycle (reauth/restart UI) lands, keep the session orchestration in this layer rather than command.

summary: Cross-layer change reviewed for layering. The previously-missing session/ layer is added in its correct position; SessionManager owns per-account API+Listener and lifecycle, command delegates to it, and zca-rust stays confined to zalo behind the MessageListener seam. The long-standing in-command session-store debt is resolved. Forward-only order intact. Approve.
